notes: 2017 Shinrin-Yoku (forest bathing) systematic review; 64 studies; confirmed: parasympathetic activation, reduced cortisol, improved HRV, blood pressure, NK cell activity, mood, anxiety, depression
2019 Frontiers in Psychology; nature and psychological well-being; attention restoration theory and stress recovery theory frameworks; useful mechanistic context
2024 meta-analysis; 59 studies; green space exposure and psychiatric disorders (depression, anxiety, dementia, schizophrenia, ADHD); OR=0.91 for psychiatric disorders overall

Twohig-Bennett & Jones 2019, Environmental Research; 103 observational + 40 interventional studies; ~100 outcomes; nature exposure reduces salivary cortisol, heart rate, diastolic BP (-1.97mmHg), T2D risk, all-cause and CVD mortality
